title: Heddal

infobox.value: is a [former municipality](/wiki/List_of_former_municipalities_of_Norway "List of former municipalities of Norway") in [Telemark](/wiki/Telemark "Telemark") county, [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). The 407-square-kilometre (157 sq mi)[[5]](#cite_note-NLF-5) municipality existed from 1838 until its dissolution in 1964. The area is now part of [Notodden Municipality](/wiki/Notodden_Municipality "Notodden Municipality"). The [administrative centre](/wiki/Administrative_centre "Administrative centre") was the village of [Heddal](/wiki/Heddal_(village) "Heddal (village)") where the [Heddal Stave Church](/wiki/Heddal_Stave_Church "Heddal Stave Church").[[6]](#cite_note-snl-6)

**Heddal** is a [former municipality](/wiki/List_of_former_municipalities_of_Norway "List of former municipalities of Norway") in [Telemark](/wiki/Telemark "Telemark") county, [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). The 407-square-kilometre (157 sq mi)[[5]](#cite_note-NLF-5) municipality existed from 1838 until its dissolution in 1964. The area is now part of [Notodden Municipality](/wiki/Notodden_Municipality "Notodden Municipality"). The [administrative centre](/wiki/Administrative_centre "Administrative centre") was the village of [Heddal](/wiki/Heddal_(village) "Heddal (village)") where the [Heddal Stave Church](/wiki/Heddal_Stave_Church "Heddal Stave Church").[[6]](#cite_note-snl-6)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heddal&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/23/Notodden_heddal_Ryen_nedre_RK_86715_medieval_IMG_9916.JPG/250px-Notodden_heddal_Ryen_nedre_RK_86715_medieval_IMG_9916.JPG)](/wiki/File:Notodden_heddal_Ryen_nedre_RK_86715_medieval_IMG_9916.JPG)

Farm area in Heddal

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f7/Johannes_Flintoe_-_Draktstudier_fra_Heddal_i_Telemark_-_NG.K%26H.1984.0043_-_National_Museum_of_Art%2C_Architecture_and_Design.jpg/250px-Johannes_Flintoe_-_Draktstudier_fra_Heddal_i_Telemark_-_NG.K%26H.1984.0043_-_National_Museum_of_Art%2C_Architecture_and_Design.jpg)](/wiki/File:Johannes_Flintoe_-_Draktstudier_fra_Heddal_i_Telemark_-_NG.K%26H.1984.0043_-_National_Museum_of_Art,_Architecture_and_Design.jpg)

Traditional 19th century [bunads](/wiki/Bunad "Bunad") from Heddal

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9a/Heddal_og_Notodden_Museumslag-1.jpg/250px-Heddal_og_Notodden_Museumslag-1.jpg)](/wiki/File:Heddal_og_Notodden_Museumslag-1.jpg)

Historic farm buildings in Heddal

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d5/142._Thelemarken%2C_Hotel_Furuheim_i_Hiterdal_-_NB_bldsa_AL0142.jpg/250px-142._Thelemarken%2C_Hotel_Furuheim_i_Hiterdal_-_NB_bldsa_AL0142.jpg)](/wiki/File:142._Thelemarken,_Hotel_Furuheim_i_Hiterdal_-_NB_bldsa_AL0142.jpg)

Hotel Furuheim in Heddal (c. 1885)

The [parish](/wiki/Prestegjeld "Prestegjeld") of *Hitterdal* (later spelled *Heddal*) was established as a municipality on 1 January 1838 (see [formannskapsdistrikt](/wiki/Formannskapsdistrikt "Formannskapsdistrikt") law). The municipality originally consisted of two parishes: Hitterdal with the [Hitterdal Stave Church](/wiki/Heddal_Stave_Church "Heddal Stave Church") and Lilleherred with the [Lilleherred Church](/wiki/Lisleherad_Church "Lisleherad Church") (the spellings of the churches and parishes have varied historically). On 1 January 1913, the newly-designated [kjøpstad](/wiki/Kj%C3%B8pstad "Kjøpstad") (town) of [Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_(town) "Notodden (town)") (population: 4,821) was separated from Heddal to form a separate self-governing town as an [enclave](/wiki/Enclave "Enclave") within Heddal Municipality. This left Heddal with 2,890 residents.[[7]](#cite_note-Dag-7)

During the 1960s, there were many municipal mergers across Norway due to the work of the [Schei Committee](/wiki/Schei_Committee "Schei Committee"). On 1 January 1964, Heddal Municipality was dissolved and the following areas were merged to form a new, larger [Notodden Municipality](/wiki/Notodden_Municipality "Notodden Municipality"):[[7]](#cite_note-Dag-7)

* most of [Gransherad Municipality](/wiki/Gransherad "Gransherad") (population: 1,115) - except for the upper [Jondalen](/wiki/Jondalen "Jondalen") valley which became part of [Kongsberg Municipality](/wiki/Kongsberg_Municipality "Kongsberg Municipality")
* all of Heddal Municipality (population: 4,844)
* the [Rudsgrendi](/wiki/Rudsgrendi "Rudsgrendi") area of [Hovin Municipality](/wiki/Hovin,_Telemark "Hovin, Telemark") (population: 21)
* the [town of Notodden](/wiki/Notodden_(town) "Notodden (town)") (population: 7,523)

### Name

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heddal&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Name")]

The municipality (originally the [parish](/wiki/Prestegjeld "Prestegjeld")) is named after the Heddalen valley ([Old Norse](/wiki/Old_Norse_language "Old Norse language"): *Heitrardalr*) since the historic [Heddal Stave Church](/wiki/Heddal_Stave_Church "Heddal Stave Church") was built there. The first element is derived from the old name for the local river [Heddøla](/wiki/Hedd%C3%B8la "Heddøla"), a tributary of the [Skien River](/wiki/Skien_River "Skien River"). The old name of the river comes from the [genitive case](/wiki/Genitive_case "Genitive case") of the word *heitr* which has an unknown meaning. The last element is *[dalr](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/dalr#Old_Norse "wikt:dalr")* which means "[valley](/wiki/Valley "Valley")" or "dale".[[8]](#cite_note-8) Historically, the name of the municipality was spelled *Hitterdal* or *Hiterdal*. On 3 November 1917, a [royal resolution](/wiki/Royal_decree "Royal decree") changed the spelling of the name of the municipality to *Heddal*.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heddal&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Government")]

During its existence, this municipality was governed by a [municipal council](/wiki/Municipal_council_(Norway) "Municipal council (Norway)") of [directly elected](/wiki/Direct_election "Direct election") representatives. The [mayor](/wiki/Mayor "Mayor") was [indirectly elected](/wiki/Indirect_election "Indirect election") by a vote of the municipal council.[[10]](#cite_note-ks-10)

### Municipal council

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heddal&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Municipal council")]

The [municipal council](/wiki/Municipal_council_(Norway) "Municipal council (Norway)") *(Herredsstyre)* of Heddal was made up of representatives that were elected to four year terms. The tables below show the historical composition of the council by political [party](/wiki/Political_party "Political party").

| Party name *(in Norwegian)* | | Number of representatives |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(Norway) "Labour Party (Norway)") *(Arbeiderpartiet)* | 12 |
|  | [Conservative Party](/wiki/Conservative_Party_(Norway) "Conservative Party (Norway)") *(Høyre)* | 1 |
|  | [Communist Party](/wiki/Communist_Party_of_Norway "Communist Party of Norway") *(Kommunistiske Parti)* | 1 |
|  | [Christian Democratic Party](/wiki/Christian_Democratic_Party_(Norway) "Christian Democratic Party (Norway)") *(Kristelig Folkeparti)* | 2 |
|  | [Centre Party](/wiki/Centre_Party_(Norway) "Centre Party (Norway)") *(Senterpartiet)* | 4 |
|  | [Liberal Party](/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Norway) "Liberal Party (Norway)") *(Venstre)* | 1 |
|  | [List of workers, fishermen, and small farmholders](/wiki/Bygdeliste "Bygdeliste") *(Arbeidere, fiskere, småbrukere liste)* | 8 |

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Heddal&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Egil Bergsland](/wiki/Egil_Bergsland "Egil Bergsland") (1924–2007), a Norwegian politician for the Labour Party
* [Thorolf Bugge](/wiki/Thorolf_Bugge "Thorolf Bugge") (1879–1935), a Norwegian trade unionist and politician
* [Olea Crøger](/wiki/Olea_Cr%C3%B8ger "Olea Crøger") (1801–1855), a Folklore collector who published old folk tunes
* [Sigmund Groven](/wiki/Sigmund_Groven "Sigmund Groven") (born 1946), a Norwegian classical harmonica player
* [Hans Herbjørnsrud](/wiki/Hans_Herbj%C3%B8rnsrud "Hans Herbjørnsrud") (1938–2023), an author of short stories.
